description: Florida homeowners face an average of 3.5 power outages per year, primarily from hurricanes and tropical storms. With summer temperatures regularly exceeding 95°F, losing air conditioning during an outage isn't just uncomfortable — it can be dangerous. A whole-house generator ensures your home stays powered through hurricane season and beyond.

After Winter Storm Uri left millions without power for days in 2021, Texas homeowners know that backup power isn't optional. Between hurricane season on the Gulf Coast and increasingly unstable winter weather, a home generator protects your family year-round.

Louisiana experiences some of the longest power outages in the nation, averaging over 13 hours per interruption. From Hurricane Katrina to Hurricane Ida, Louisiana residents know that reliable backup power can be the difference between safety and disaster.

South Carolina recorded nearly 53 hours of power outages in 2024 — the highest of any state in the nation. Hurricane Helene alone left over 1.2 million customers without power. For Lowcountry and coastal residents, a standby generator is essential.

North Carolina faces a dual threat: hurricanes along the coast and ice storms in the Piedmont and mountains. Both can leave homes without power for days. A whole-house generator keeps your family comfortable and safe regardless of the season.

Georgia's mix of severe thunderstorms, occasional tornadoes, and remnants of Gulf Coast hurricanes creates a persistent power outage risk. Metro Atlanta's aging tree canopy makes the grid especially vulnerable during storms.

California homeowners face a unique challenge: Public Safety Power Shutoffs (PSPS). Utilities proactively cut power during high fire-risk conditions, leaving homes dark for days. Generator installations in California are more expensive due to seismic bracing requirements and strict permitting.

Michigan's harsh winters bring ice storms and heavy snow that frequently down power lines. DTE Energy and Consumers Energy customers are no strangers to multi-day outages during winter storms. A natural gas standby generator keeps your home heated and lit.

From Superstorm Sandy to annual nor'easters, New York homeowners — especially on Long Island and in Westchester — know that power outages are a matter of when, not if. Installation costs are higher in the tri-state area, but so is the peace of mind.

Tennessee sits in Dixie Alley — one of the most tornado-prone regions in the country. Nashville's 2020 tornado and recurring severe thunderstorms have driven demand for home generators to an all-time high across the state.
